description_html:
This Valorant tournament features competitive online group stages followed by offline play-offs. Teams will compete across multiple matches using a structured Best of 3 and Best of 5 format, emphasizing strategy, coordination, and consistency. Strict technical and conduct rules are enforced to ensure a fair and professional competitive environment.

Group Stages:

  • Two groups: Group A and Group B.
  • Group stage matches are Best of 3.
  • Each team gets to play 3 games in the group stage.
  • Top 2 teams from each group will be selected for the Play-offs.
  • Group stage matches are conducted online.

Play-Offs:

  • Play-off matches are Best of 3.
  • Finals are Best of 5.
  • Refer fixtures for details.
  • Play-offs are conducted offline on campus.

Best of 3 Format:

  • Team A bans a map.
  • Team B bans a map.
  • Team A picks a map.
  • Team B picks a map.
  • Team A bans a map.
  • Team B bans a map.
  • Team A chooses sides.

Best of 5 Format:

  • The number 1 team entering the finals gets both initial map bans.
  • Remaining maps are picked alternately.

Map selection order:

  • Team A bans a map.
  • Team B bans a map.
  • Team A picks a map.
  • Team B picks a map.
  • Team A picks a map.
  • Team B picks a map.
  • Team A chooses sides.

Map Pool:

  • Heaven
  • Abyss
  • Bind
  • Split
  • Corrode
  • Lotus
  • Breeze

General Rules:

  • Outside Participants Are Allowed.
  • Internet Connection Must Be Through Lan Cable Only. Use of Wi-Fi, Routers, Modems, or Similar Devices Is Not Allowed.
  • All Players Must Use the Same Server (mumbai) Throughout the Matches to Ensure Fair Play.
  • Team Captains Must Ensure Their Teams Are In-Game Within 15 Minutes of The Scheduled Match Time.
  • Failure to Do so Will Result in A Forfeit, Counted as A 13-0 Defeat.
  • For Play-Offs Held Offline on Campus, the Complete Team Must Be Present.
  • Participants Must Bring Their Own Setups, Including Lan Cables and Extension Boxes.
  • No Cheats Are Allowed.
  • Use of Newly Released Agents Is Not Allowed.
  • Any Disrespectful Behavior or Trash Talk May Lead to Immediate Disqualification.
  • Tournament Mode Must Be On, with Overtime Enabled to Decide the Winner.
  • Each Win Awards One Point.
  • Tie-Breakers Are Decided by The Total Number of Rounds Won.
  • The Match Will Be Paused Once at The Start to Allow Teams to Leave the Party Once for Voice Communication.
  • Each Team Is Allowed Two Timeouts per Map.
  • Normal Map Bans Apply, and The Starting Team Will Be Decided by A Toss.
  • Winning Teams Must Share a Final Scoreboard Screenshot Clearly Displaying Kills per Player.
  • The Screenshot Must Include a Caption in The Following Format:
  • Failure to Follow the Above Format Will Result in The Win Not Being Counted.
  • The organizers’ decision is final in case of any disputes or issues.
